description_long: This is a mid-rise brick office building with five stories, featuring a flat roof topped by a cylindrical, rusted water tower. The front facade is adorned with arched white window frames and a prominent metal fire escape running vertically between the windows. The ground floor includes a recessed entryway and large windows, while the upper floors have regularly spaced, rectangular windows. The side facades are simpler, with one side displaying a large section of light gray brick and a vertical row of windows, while the rear facade is entirely brown brick with a grid of windows. The building sits on a concrete base, and air conditioning units are visible on several windows. The overall appearance suggests early 20th-century industrial or warehouse conversion architecture, commonly found in dense urban environments.
description: A five-story brick office building with arched windows, a rooftop water tower, and a metal fire escape on the front facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing arched windows, a metal fire escape, and the main entrance at the base.
description_view_1: Side view with a large light gray brick section and a vertical row of windows, plus the rooftop water tower.
description_view_2: Rear view with a uniform grid of rectangular windows set in brown brick and no visible entrances.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, with light gray brick and a vertical window row.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced, vertically aligned rectangular windows with arched tops on the front; grid pattern on the rear; single vertical rows on the sides.
